title: erigeron compositus var. incertus
intro: Erigeron compositus var. incertus, also known as the Fleabane or Prairie Fleabane, is a perennial plant species within the sunflower family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The plant's leaves and flowers have been employed in herbal preparations to alleviate various health issues, such as fever, coughs, and digestive problems, and to support immune function and wound healing. Erigeron compositus var. incertus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and its roots have been used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory infections and skin conditions.
